description:
Goal
Eliminate unnecessary cloning, copying, ownership churn, and early materialization across the compiler/runtime pipeline by fixing ownership boundaries at their source instead of shaving isolated `.clone()` calls.

Output contract
- Each fact, value, string, and collection has one clear owning authority.
- Downstream stages borrow authority-owned state or consume it once.
- Telemetry observes existing state and does not force formatting, cloning, or derived allocations.
- Artifact stages do not re-materialize information earlier stages already own.
- Dump/debug paths stay off the hot path and do only the work required for their user-visible output.

Why
The current waste is not random style noise. It clusters around a few boundary failures:
- front-door and CLI APIs that take owned strings and force call-site cloning
- fact/job APIs that require owned keys for reads and lookups
- compiler2 jobs that clone state before or after publishing it into world storage
- lowering/planning provenance paths that may duplicate vectors instead of deriving them from authoritative state
- dump/debug code that materializes strings and collections earlier than necessary

Scope
This epic audits and repairs those boundaries structurally. It is not a sweep of local "cleanup" patches. The work should remove code and duplicate representations where possible, not add adapters, shims, caches, or compatibility scaffolding.

Plan
1. Normalize front-door and CLI ownership so borrowed inputs stay borrowed until a true owner retains them.
2. Redesign fact/job read APIs to accept borrowed keys; only durable read/wait/output sets own keys.
3. Enforce "define, then re-borrow" across compiler2 publication paths so telemetry and downstream consumers observe stored state instead of clone-preserved locals.
4. Audit lowering/planning provenance duplication and delete redundant stored data where it can be derived from authoritative IR or world state.
5. Clean dump/debug formatting paths after hot-path ownership boundaries are fixed.
6. Ratchet clone/conversion lints so the invariant stays enforced.

Validation
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -W clippy::redundant_clone -W clippy::map_clone -W clippy::iter_cloned_collect -W clippy::implicit_clone -W clippy::useless_conversion -W clippy::unnecessary_to_owned`
- `cargo test --workspace`
- targeted telemetry-observed tests for each touched boundary
- docs updated where ownership or telemetry invariants become simpler or more explicit

Removal targets
- clone-driven front-door/path plumbing
- clone-driven FactKey lookup helpers and call sites
- telemetry-induced store-local duplication
- redundant provenance/state copies that can be derived instead
- intermediate formatting/materialization used only for ordering or pass-through debug output

Ticket DAG
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Front-door and CLI ownership normalization]
    B[Borrow-first FactKey read API]
    C[Compiler2 define-then-reborrow cleanup]
    D[Lowering and planning provenance deduplication]
    E[Dump and debug path cleanup]
    F[Lint ratchet and invariant docs]

    A --> C
    B --> C
    C --> D
    C --> E
    D --> F
    E --> F
```

Execution notes
- One child ticket per structural seam, one commit per ticket.
- Prefer changing signatures and ownership authority over removing individual `.clone()` expressions in place.
- If a proposed fix requires a shim, adapter, compatibility layer, or duplicate representation, step back and redesign the boundary.
title: Ownership boundary audit: remove unnecessary cloning, copying, and conversions
